meaning: adj.没有观察到的,无人遵守的
examples: It's not easy for somebody to get into the building unobserved.
Looking around to make sure he was unobserved, he slipped through the door.
He took his seat in an unobserved corner of the assembly room.
They treat horrifying statistical conditions such as unobserved heterogeneity, selection bias, heteroscedasticity, and autocorrelation.
It was dawn by the time the tiger left the city, but he did not go unobserved.
